source_notes.official_book: NBA official scorer report uploaded by user: 20260515_DETCLE_book (1).pdf
source_notes.recap: AP recap supplied by user: Cade Cunningham scores 21 and Pistons dominate 2nd half to beat Cavs 115-94 and force Game 7.

market_positions.selection: Yes - Cleveland wins by over 3.5 points

turning_points: Cleveland led by 6 in the first quarter but committed 8 first-quarter turnovers, keeping Detroit alive despite Cleveland shooting 58.8%.
Detroit led 54-51 at halftime even while committing 11 turnovers, because Cleveland also had 13 turnovers and failed to create clean flow.
Detroit opened the third quarter with a decisive surge, turning a 3-point halftime lead into a double-digit control state.
Cleveland scored only 19 points in the third quarter on 25.0% shooting and 16.7% from three.
Mobley missed a dunk late in the third and Marcus Sasser scored at the buzzer, pushing Detroit to an 84-70 lead after three.
Detroit won the second half 61-43 and closed the fourth quarter 31-24, eliminating any Cleveland comeback path.

Cade Cunningham.model_relevance: Primary creation hub remained volatile with 7 turnovers, but his three-point shot and table-setting sustained Detroit offense.

Jalen Duren.model_relevance: Validated Duren rim pressure and offensive glass dominance. His second-chance impact was one of the core Game 6 separators.

Paul Reed.model_relevance: Bench-big minutes created extra rebound pressure and scoring depth. Add BENCH_BIG_MINUTES_SPIKE impact for Game 7.

Duncan Robinson.model_relevance: Return from back soreness restored Detroit spacing; his gravity widened driving lanes and punished Cleveland help defense.

Daniss Jenkins.model_relevance: Secondary shot-making was underpriced pregame; Game 7 model should include role-player perimeter volatility boost.

James Harden.model_relevance: Scoring held, but turnover volatility again directly damaged Cleveland team flow and live total upside.

Donovan Mitchell.model_relevance: Failed to produce alpha scoring separation in elimination response spot; downgrade Cleveland late-series shot creation ceiling if Detroit pressure stays legal and physical.

Evan Mobley.model_relevance: Rebound prop miss: finished 6 rebounds despite 35:43 minutes. Cleveland frontcourt rebound share split with Allen and Strus, and missed-shot profile did not funnel cleanly to Mobley.

betting_result_summary.notes: Live total under saved the card. Pregame Cleveland -3.5 and Mobley 9+ rebounds missed due to Detroit physicality, Cleveland turnover collapse, Detroit bench scoring, and rebound distribution away from Mobley.

why_it_won: Final total landed 209, clearing the under position by 1.5 points.
Cleveland offensive efficiency collapsed after halftime.
Detroit controlled the game state, reducing late urgency after building a large lead.
Cleveland's 20 turnovers killed possession quality and prevented scoring continuity.

missed_reads.failure_reason: Model overweighted Cleveland home response and underweighted Detroit elimination-game physicality, bench scoring, offensive rebounding, and perimeter variance.
Mobley minutes were strong but rebound funnel did not isolate to him. Allen grabbed 8, Strus grabbed 8, and Detroit shot efficiently enough to reduce clean defensive rebound volume.

detroit: Upgrade Detroit bench scoring volatility if Reed, Robinson, Sasser remain in active rhythm.
Keep Cade creation strong but turnover-risk attached.
Duren rim pressure remains a core series edge.
cleveland: Apply turnover dampener to projected team total and spread confidence.
Avoid Mobley rebound overs unless line drops or Allen/Strus rebound role changes.
Mitchell/Harden scoring can hit but does not automatically convert to team efficiency.
Cleveland assist rate is a red flag after only 15 assists on 30 made field goals.
